gates.title: Public/private boundary
gates.blockedWhenMissing: Repository remains private; public transition is NO-GO.
evidence: GitHub metadata shows repository is still private before approval.
Docs and examples contain no private endpoints, provider identifiers, raw session dumps, host-specific private paths, or runtime/bootstrap context files.
Public-facing wording describes A2A Plane as alpha/feedback-welcome, not production-ready.

gates.title: Secret/history scanner readiness
gates.blockedWhenMissing: External scanner evidence is absent or unavailable; public transition is NO-GO.
evidence: At least one supported external scanner, such as gitleaks or trufflehog, ran against the candidate tree/history.
Findings are clean or explicitly dispositioned by the operator using redacted metadata only.
Scanner evidence postdates the last commit touching secrets-adjacent paths.

gates.title: OpenClaw runtime/bootstrap hygiene
gates.blockedWhenMissing: Runtime/bootstrap context files would enter the branch or evidence; stop before PR/Done evidence.
evidence: Artifact and PR/issue evidence exclude all denyPaths and raw runtime/session context.

gates.title: GO/NO-GO matrix
gates.blockedWhenMissing: Any unresolved required gate leaves the aggregate decision NO-GO.
evidence: Each required gate has a named owner, status, evidence link, and timestamp.
The aggregate decision is GO only when every required gate is GO.

gates.title: Redacted evidence policy
gates.blockedWhenMissing: Evidence contains raw secrets, private paths, provider identifiers, terminal ACK mutations, or raw session dumps; stop and post Block evidence only.
evidence: Issue and PR evidence includes commands, exit statuses, finding counts/classes, commit SHA, and linked artifacts only.
Matched secret values, host-specific private paths, provider IDs, Telegram IDs, and raw session dumps are never pasted.

gates.title: Explicit operator approval
gates.blockedWhenMissing: No explicit operator approval for public repository visibility; public transition is NO-GO.
evidence: Approval is a linked issue/PR comment from the operator and explicitly names repository visibility/publication.
Approval is separate from execution and does not bundle deploys, restarts, DB mutations, provider sends, terminal ACKs, secret rotation/disclosure, history rewrite, or force-push.
